The Sun's rays, which are brilliant, pure, straight, long and
turned in a clock-wise direction, and which are seen in a bright
sky, presage prosperity to the world.
groi: aRi few. t
snsgfeRt imu
The Sun's rays which are white, glossy, unbroken and straight,
and which pervade the entire region of the firmament (from the
beginning to the end), are termed Amogha 'Unerring1. Such rays
indicate good rain.
Rays that are greyish, tawny, russet, variegated, madder-hued,
green and spotted, and stretch all over the sky (and are turned
upwards) are productive of drought and some danger after a
week.
[In the first Hne^^T TT is another reading.]
